breaking_news_suggestions.headline: We Add Test 7: Dome's Stellar Parallax Prediction Rejected at 6,281 Sigma
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: The dome predicts all stars should show identical 0.454 arcsec parallax from firmament wobble (WIN-017). Gaia measured 1.8 billion stars and found parallax varying by a factor of 760, tracking stellar distance exactly as orbital geometry requires. Proxima Centauri alone rejects the dome prediction at 6,281σ — the most statistically devastating single test in the entire review. New Horizons independently confirmed from 7 billion km (physically impossible under the dome model).
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: 6,281σ is exceptional even by physics standards. New Horizons confirmation from 'above the dome' is a clean structural impossibility argument. Makes Part 5 significantly stronger.
